spaSim3D 0.1.0

Simulate with interactive functions

Source: vignettes/simulate_with_interactive_functions.Rmd
simulate_with_interactive_functions.Rmd

spaSim3D has two interactive functions which allow the user to generate 3D tissue simulations at their own pace. When running each function, you will be asked to input specific inputs directly into the console to simulate 3D tissues. The meaning of each input should be clearly described as you work through the functions, so I won’t explain them in this vignette.

Start by generating the ‘background’ cells for your 3D tissue using simulate_background_interactive().

Then, generate 3D cell clusters on top of your background using simulate_cluster_interactive().

That’s it. You can keep adding cell clusters, or start from scratch.

I think these functions are great for users who have only just encountered this package for the first time. However, for large scale simulation projects, an interactive approach would be very slow, and I would recommend the other vignettes which show other approaches for simulation instead.

### First, generate the background
# background_spe <- simulate_background_interactive()

### Add a cluster to the background
# cluster_spe <- simulate_cluster_interactive(background_spe)

### Add another cluster
# cluster_spe <- simulate_cluster_interactive(cluster_spe)

Developed by David Le.
